title: Implement wai ls: cross-project global workspace view
description:
Tool work: implement the `wai ls` top-level CLI command per openspec/changes/add-ls-command/. Requires approved openspec before merging.

## What
New `wai ls` command that scans for wai workspaces under a root directory (default: $HOME, depth: 3) and shows each project's phase and open issue count as a one-line table.

Expected output:
```
why-command   [review]    3 open, 2 ready
para          [plan]      7 open, 0 ready
rizomas       [implement] 1 open, 1 ready
```

## Key design decisions (see openspec/changes/add-ls-command/design.md)
- Traversal: use walkdir with follow_links(false); check for .wai/config.toml per directory; skip hidden dirs; do NOT descend into .wai/ itself
- Counts column: global toggle — shown for ALL rows when any workspace has beads, blank cell for non-beads rows, omitted entirely when no workspace has beads; counts are per-workspace (one bd invocation per workspace), shared across all project rows from that workspace
- Duplicate names: append path suffix when two projects share a name: `name (~/path/to/repo)`
- dirs and walkdir crates confirmed in Cargo.toml

## Tasks (from openspec/changes/add-ls-command/tasks.md)

### 1. CLI definition
- [ ] 1.1 Add `Ls { root: Option<PathBuf>, depth: Option<usize> }` to `Commands` enum in `src/cli.rs`
- [ ] 1.2 Add `"ls"` to `valid_commands` array in `run_external()` in `src/commands/mod.rs`

### 2. Workspace discovery
- [ ] 2.1 Add `discover_workspaces(root, max_depth) -> Vec<PathBuf>` using walkdir with follow_links(false); for each directory check if .wai/config.toml exists within it (match = wai workspace); do NOT recurse into .wai/ itself; skip all other hidden directories during traversal
- [ ] 2.2 For each workspace, enumerate project names by listing subdirectories of `.wai/projects/` via `std::fs::read_dir()` — use the `projects_dir()` helper from `src/config.rs`. IMPORTANT: `.wai/config.toml` holds only the workspace name (`[project] name`), NOT a list of projects; do not try to read project names from config.toml
- [ ] 2.3 For each project, read phase using `ProjectState::load()` from `src/state.rs` (reads `.wai/projects/<project>/.state` as YAML, `current` field); default to `unknown` on missing file or parse error (ProjectState::load already handles missing files gracefully)

### 3. Plugin integration (beads, when detected)
- [ ] 3.1 If `.beads/` exists in the workspace directory, invoke `bd stats --json` to get `open` and `ready` counts; skip gracefully if `bd` is not installed or exits non-zero
- [ ] 3.2 Store counts at workspace level; apply the same open/ready counts to all project rows from that workspace when rendering (bd is invoked once per workspace, not once per project — all projects in a beads-enabled workspace share the same counts)

### 4. Command implementation
- [ ] 4.1 Create `src/commands/ls.rs`
- [ ] 4.2 Resolve root: use `--root <path>` if provided (fail with diagnostic if path does not exist), else `$HOME` via `dirs::home_dir()`; fail with diagnostic if home directory cannot be determined
- [ ] 4.3 Resolve depth: use `--depth <n>` if provided, else default to 3
- [ ] 4.4 Run workspace discovery, collect results sorted by project name
- [ ] 4.5 Render table: one line per (workspace, project) pair with columns left-aligned and padded to longest name; show counts column for ALL rows when any workspace has beads data (blank cell for non-beads rows); omit counts column entirely when no workspace has beads; append `(~/path)` suffix to disambiguate duplicate project names
- [ ] 4.6 Print `No wai workspaces found under <root>` when discovery returns nothing

### 5. Wire up
- [ ] 5.1 Dispatch `Commands::Ls` in `src/commands/mod.rs`
- [ ] 5.2 Verify `wai ls --help` output describes the command and references `--root` and `--depth`

### 6. Tests
- [ ] 6.1 Integration test: root with one workspace and one project → one-line output with correct phase
- [ ] 6.2 Integration test: root with multiple workspaces → all projects shown, sorted by name
- [ ] 6.3 Integration test: no workspaces found under root → empty-output message
- [ ] 6.4 Integration test: workspace with beads detected → counts column shown for all rows; blank cell for non-beads rows
- [ ] 6.5 Integration test: no workspace has beads → counts column omitted entirely
- [ ] 6.6 Integration test: `--depth 1` stops recursion at the expected level
- [ ] 6.7 Integration test: `--root /nonexistent` → diagnostic error with the invalid path

## References
- Openspec: openspec/changes/add-ls-command/
- Design: openspec/changes/add-ls-command/design.md
- Data model: src/config.rs (projects_dir, ProjectConfig), src/state.rs (ProjectState)
- Reference implementation: src/commands/status.rs (project enumeration pattern at line ~30)
- Design ticket closed: wai-ycb
close_reason: wai ls implemented per openspec/changes/add-ls-command/ — all 22 tasks complete, 7 integration tests pass

title: wai why: Phase 2 - Context Gathering
description:
Implement the context gathering layer for wai why.

## Tasks

- [ ] 2.1 Implement context gatherer in src/commands/why.rs
- [ ] 2.2 Read all artifacts from .wai/ (research, design, plan, handoff)
- [ ] 2.3 Implement robust file path detection (exists, starts with ./, src/, contains / without spaces)
- [ ] 2.4 Gather git blame/log with graceful failure handling (check git available, file tracked)
- [ ] 2.5 Collect project metadata (current phase, recent commits)
- [ ] 2.6 Implement empty artifact warning (zero artifacts → show message)
- [ ] 2.7 Implement context size management (>100K tokens → truncate to 50 most recent)

## Architecture

The ContextGatherer component: reads all artifacts fresh on each query (no indexing), file path detection heuristics, truncation prioritizes: (1) artifacts mentioning query terms, (2) most recent, (3) at least one of each type.

title: Add Autonomous Loop section to managed_block.rs
description:
In the wai_block_content() Rust string in src/managed_block.rs, find the '## Ending a Session' push_str block and append the Autonomous Loop subsection unconditionally (no plugin detection guard).

Content to add (verbatim from openspec/changes/add-resume-loop/design.md § Autonomous Loop Content):

### Autonomous Loop

One task per session. The resume loop:

1. `wai prime` — orient (shows ⚡ RESUMING if mid-task)
2. Work on the single task
3. `wai close` — capture state (run this before every `/clear`)
4. `git add <files> && git commit`
5. `/clear` — fresh context

→ Next session: `wai prime` shows RESUMING with exact next steps.

When context reaches ~40%: run `wai close`, then `/clear`.
Do NOT skip `wai close` — it enables resume detection.

Spec: openspec/changes/add-resume-loop/
Task: 4.1

title: Fix compiler warnings during `just install`
description:
Building with `just install` produces 19 compiler warnings (13 lib + 6 bin, with some overlap). Categories:

**Unused imports (3):**
- `outro` in src/guided_flows.rs:1
- `crate::config::mark_tutorial_seen` in src/guided_flows.rs:4
- `std::path::Path` in src/commands/sync.rs:5

**Unused struct field assignments in error.rs (10):**
These are 'value assigned to X is never read' warnings on thiserror enum fields. Likely the fields are declared but not referenced in the `#[error(...)]` format string. Fix by either using them in the Display impl or removing them.
- `path` in ProjectExists (line 28)
- `name` in ProjectNotFound (line 35)
- `name` in AreaNotFound (line 42)
- `name` in ResourceNotFound (line 49)
- `to` in move variant (line 58)
- `message` in ConfigSyncError (line 74)
- `message` in HandoffError (line 78)
- `name` in PluginNotFound (line 85)
- `message` in NonInteractive (line 92)
- `action` in SafeModeViolation (line 99)

**Unused variables (1):**
- `content` in src/guided_flows.rs:28 (assigned from input but never used)

**Dead code (3):**
- `is_first_run` in src/config.rs:222
- `run_non_interactive` in src/tutorial.rs:96
- `should_show_tutorial` in src/tutorial.rs:117

**Approach:**
- Unused imports: remove them (`cargo fix` can handle these)
- error.rs fields: include them in the `#[error(...)]` format strings so error messages are informative
- Unused variable: either use `content` or prefix with `_`
- Dead code: review whether these are needed for upcoming features; if not, remove them
close_reason: All 19 compiler warnings fixed. 0 warnings on dev, release, and test builds. 91 tests pass.

title: Implement wai close: session-end helper command
description:
Tool work: implement the `wai close` top-level CLI command per openspec/changes/add-close-command/. Requires approved openspec before merging.

## What
New `wai close` command that automates the session-end checklist: create a handoff for the active project, surface uncommitted files, and suggest the next steps.

Expected output (the `!` line uses comma-separated bare names; quotes appear only in the `git add` suggestion):
```
✓ Handoff created: .wai/projects/<project>/handoffs/2026-02-24-session.md
! Uncommitted changes: src/main.rs, CLAUDE.md
→ Next: bd sync --from-main && git add "src/main.rs" "CLAUDE.md" && git commit
```

## Tasks (from openspec/changes/add-close-command/tasks.md)

### 1. CLI definition
- [ ] 1.1 Add `Close { project: Option<String> }` variant to `Commands` enum in `src/cli.rs`

### 2. Command implementation
- [ ] 2.1 Create `src/commands/close.rs`; note: `close.rs` formats its own `✓ Handoff created: <full-path>` success line — do NOT reuse the existing log message from `handoff.rs` (different wording)
- [ ] 2.2 Auto-detect project: fail with "no projects" diagnostic if none exist; use the single project if only one exists; prompt (cliclack select) if multiple; fail with diagnostic when `--no-input` and multiple projects
- [ ] 2.3 Extract `create_handoff(project_root: &Path, project: &str) -> Result<PathBuf>` from `src/commands/handoff.rs`; this function contains ONLY the pure handoff-creation logic — `require_safe_mode("create handoff")` stays at each call site (both `wai handoff create` and `close.rs` call it before invoking the extracted function); both `wai handoff create` and `wai close` call the extracted function
- [ ] 2.4 Read git status: shell out to `git status --porcelain`; treat non-zero exit (git not installed, not a git repo) as "no git" and skip the git section silently
- [ ] 2.5 Render uncommitted files as a single `!`-prefixed line with comma-separated bare filenames; cap at 10 files and append `… and N more` when exceeded
- [ ] 2.6 Print plugin-aware next-steps: include `bd sync --from-main &&` prefix only when beads plugin is detected; include the uncommitted filenames in the `git add` suggestion, each wrapped in double-quotes to handle paths with spaces

### 3. Wire up
- [ ] 3.1 Dispatch `Commands::Close` in `src/commands/mod.rs`
- [ ] 3.2 Add `"close"` to `valid_commands` in the external subcommand handler
- [ ] 3.3 Verify `wai close --help` output matches expected format and references `--project`

### 4. Tests
- [ ] 4.1 Integration test: single project workspace → handoff created, output matches expected format (with beads plugin detected: assert `bd sync --from-main` present in next-steps)
- [ ] 4.2 Integration test: `--project <name>` flag creates handoff without interactive prompt
- [ ] 4.3 Integration test: unknown project name → diagnostic error lists available projects
- [ ] 4.4 Integration test: zero projects workspace → diagnostic error suggests `wai new project`
- [ ] 4.5 Integration test: repeated invocation on same day → second handoff uses incremented suffix (e.g. session-end-1.md), first file not overwritten or errored

## References
- Openspec: openspec/changes/add-close-command/
- Design ticket closed: wai-t86

title: Implement wai resource import skills
description:
Bulk-import existing skills into `.wai/resources/agent-config/skills/`.

## Behaviour
1. Default source: `.agents/skills/` in workspace root
2. Optional `--from <path>` to specify alternate source
3. For each `<name>/SKILL.md` found in source:
   a. Copy entire `<name>/` directory (SKILL.md, scripts/, toolbox/, mcp.json)
   b. Skip if target already exists (warn per-skill)
4. Report: N imported, N skipped
5. Remind to run `wai sync`
6. Respect `--safe` mode

## Acceptance Criteria
- Copies full directory trees (not just SKILL.md)
- Skips existing with warning, doesn't overwrite
- Works with both .agents/skills/ and custom paths
- Handles source path not existing (clear error)
- Safe mode respected

title: Enhance doctor projection consistency checks
description:
Enhance `check_agent_config_sync()` in doctor.rs to detect content inconsistencies between source and projected targets.

## Current State
Only checks: .projections.yml parses + target paths exist.

## New Checks

### All strategies
- Warn if source directory in projections doesn't exist

### Symlink strategy
- Verify each entry in target is a symlink pointing to correct source path
- Detect broken symlinks

### Inline strategy
- Rebuild expected concatenated content in memory
- SHA-256 hash comparison against target file
- No mtime dependency (unreliable across git ops)

### Reference strategy
- Rebuild expected reference list, compare to target content

## Output
- Pass: "Projection → <target> is in sync"
- Warn: "Projection → <target> is stale (N files changed)" + fix: "Run: wai sync"
- Warn: "Projection → <target> has broken symlinks" + fix: "Run: wai sync"
- Warn: "Projection source '<dir>' not found" + fix: "Check .projections.yml sources"

- Add `sources: Vec<String>` to doctor's local `ProjectionEntry` struct
- Reuse inline/reference content building logic from sync.rs (or extract shared helper)

title: wai why: Phase 5 - Error Handling & Fallback
description:
Implement error handling, graceful degradation, and fallback behavior for wai why.

## Tasks

- [ ] 5.1 Graceful degradation to wai search if no LLM available
- [ ] 5.2 Handle LLM API errors with miette diagnostics (invalid_api_key, rate_limit, network_error, model_not_found)
- [ ] 5.3 Detect Ollama model not downloaded and suggest ollama pull
- [ ] 5.4 Handle rate limits with clear message (wait 60s or use Ollama)
- [ ] 5.5 Add --no-llm flag to force fallback for testing

## Architecture

Fallback flow: if LLM unavailable → eprintln warning + call wai_search(query). If LLM errors mid-request → eprintln warning + call wai_search(query). --no-llm flag bypasses LLM entirely (for CI, privacy, testing).

UX message patterns (miette diagnostics, owo_colors):
- No LLM: warning + remediation (install Ollama or set ANTHROPIC_API_KEY)
- No artifacts: warning + suggest wai add
- Git unavailable: informational
- Rate limited: clear wait message
- Ollama model missing: suggest ollama pull <model>

## References

- Design: openspec/changes/add-why-command/design.md (Decision 4: Graceful Degradation, Risks 1-4)
- Tasks: openspec/changes/add-why-command/tasks.md

CRITICAL: Use TDD and Tidy First

From user behavior research: session end neglect — many threads end with 'commit and push' with no handoff. Design a 'wai close' CLI subcommand that wraps 'wai handoff create <project>' + prints 'bd sync --from-main' reminder + shows git status. Automates the session-close checklist into one command.

This is the long-term replacement for checklist-based approaches (CLAUDE.md already has the checklist at L65, but it isn't consistently triggered — the trigger is the gap).

  $ wai close
  ✓ Handoff created: .wai/projects/why-command/handoffs/2026-02-24-session.md
  ! Uncommitted changes: src/main.rs, CLAUDE.md
  → Next: bd sync --from-main && git add <files> && git commit

Note: handoff-system spec marks automatic generation as non-goal. Hook-based triggering (user still invokes wai close) is different and compatible with the spec. Part of planned wai-ux project.

See: .wai/projects/why-command/research/2026-02-24-user-behavior-analysis-claude-amp-gemini-ope.md Gap B / Priority 4.

title: Implement wai prime: session orientation command
description:
Tool work: implement the `wai prime` top-level CLI command per openspec/changes/add-prime-command/. Requires approved openspec before merging.

## What
New `wai prime` command that gives a single at-a-glance orientation: active project, phase, last handoff summary, plugin status summaries, and suggested next action.

Expected output:
```
◆ wai prime — 2026-02-24
• Project: why-command [review]
• Handoff: 2026-02-23 — 'Completed Phase 9.1 verbosity levels...'
• Beads:   3 open issues (2 ready)
• Spec:    add-why-command: 49/54 (91%)
→ Suggested next: bd show wai-abc
```

## Key design decisions (see openspec/changes/add-prime-command/design.md)
- Plugin summaries: call `plugin::run_hooks(project_root, "on_status")` (src/commands/status.rs:89) and `openspec::read_status(project_root)` (src/commands/status.rs:92) — do NOT duplicate or shell out to wai status; re-verify line numbers at start of implementation (task 3.0)
- Suggested next: invoke `bd ready --json`, use first element's `id` field; skip silently on any error
- Handoff snippet: graceful degradation on missing/malformed frontmatter; "no summary yet" when handoff has no paragraph content

## Tasks (from openspec/changes/add-prime-command/tasks.md)

### 1. CLI definition
- [ ] 1.1 Add `Prime { project: Option<String> }` variant to `Commands` enum in `src/cli.rs`
- [ ] 1.2 Add `"prime"` to `valid_commands` in the external subcommand handler (src/commands/mod.rs)

### 2. Handoff reading

- [ ] 2.2 Add helper `read_handoff_summary(path: &Path) -> (NaiveDate, String)` that parses frontmatter date + first paragraph snippet (80 chars); gracefully degrades on malformed input; uses "no summary yet" when no paragraph found

### 3. Command implementation
- [ ] 3.0 Verify plugin hook entry points in src/commands/status.rs before implementing (expected: plugin::run_hooks at line ~89, openspec::read_status at line ~92)
- [ ] 3.1 Create `src/commands/prime.rs`

- [ ] 3.5 Get suggested next via `bd ready --json`, parse first element's `id`; skip silently on failure
- [ ] 3.6 Render prime view (YYYY-MM-DD local clock, phase inline in Project bullet)

### 4. Wire up
- [ ] 4.1 Dispatch `Commands::Prime` in `src/commands/mod.rs`
- [ ] 4.2 Verify `wai prime --help` output describes the command and references `--project`

### 5. Tests
- [ ] 5.1 Integration test: single project with handoff → full output rendered correctly
- [ ] 5.2 Integration test: no handoff exists → handoff line omitted, rest intact
- [ ] 5.3 Integration test: `--project <name>` selects correct project without prompting
- [ ] 5.4 Integration test: zero projects workspace → diagnostic error suggesting `wai new project`

- [ ] 3.2 Support API key from config api_key field OR ANTHROPIC_API_KEY env var
- [ ] 3.3 Implement Ollama local model client (process spawn, optional)
- [ ] 3.4 Implement backend selection priority (explicit config > auto-detect)
- [ ] 3.5 Auto-detect available LLM backend (Claude API responds, ollama model available)
- [ ] 3.6 Build prompt template with gathered context and artifact escaping
- [ ] 3.7 Support model aliases ("haiku" → "claude-haiku-3-5", "sonnet" → "claude-sonnet-4")

## Architecture

LLMClient trait:
  fn complete(&self, prompt: &str) -> Result<String>
  fn is_available(&self) -> bool

Implementors: ClaudeClient (API via reqwest), OllamaClient (process spawn).

Backend selection priority:
1. Explicit config [why] llm = "..." takes precedence
2. Auto-detect: ANTHROPIC_API_KEY + responds → Claude; ollama binary + model available → Ollama
3. Fall back to wai search if none available

Prompt template structure: role (oracle), user question, project context (phase + commits), available artifacts, git context (file queries only), task instructions (find 3-5 relevant artifacts, synthesize narrative).

title: Design wai ls --global: cross-project view
description:
Tool work: new CLI subcommand or flag in src/. Requires openspec proposal before implementation (openspec/AGENTS.md). This ticket covers the design stage — resolve the design questions below before writing any Rust.

From user behavior research: wai status is per-repo. No way to see all projects using wai and their phase/open issues at a glance. Design 'wai ls' (or 'wai status --global' flag) that scans for .wai/ directories and reports phase + open issue count per project.

Key design questions to resolve before implementation:
- Scan strategy: walk upward from cwd? Scan a configured list of roots in ~/.wai/config.toml? Both?
- Performance: what if there are 50+ repos? Should scan be lazy/cached?
- Output: one line per repo or a structured table?
- Scope: only repos the current user owns, or any .wai/ found on path?

Suggested output sketch:
  why-command   [review]   3 open, 2 ready
  para          [plan]     7 open, 0 ready
  rizomas       [implement] 1 open, 1 ready

Genuine new capability with no existing equivalent.

See: .wai/projects/why-command/research/2026-02-24-user-behavior-analysis-claude-amp-gemini-ope.md Gap E / Priority 6.
close_reason: Design questions resolved (scan from $HOME depth=3, --root/--depth flags, one line per project, beads counts when available). Openspec proposal add-ls-command scaffolded and validated (openspec/changes/add-ls-command/). Implementation is a follow-on ticket.
